rag_rules: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; move valid bindings to implementation annex or remove invalid guarantees.
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types and resolve to visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the input AADL model.
Do not generate AGREE clauses that reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens as variables.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions are type-consistent and refer only to visible identifiers.
Place component type contracts in type scope and implementation bindings in implementation scope.
Use only declared helper functions or visible AADL features in AGREE expressions.
Remove or rewrite clauses that reference unresolved or invalid identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantee statements are only allowed in component types, not implementations.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must evaluate to type bool.
Helper declarations must match the types of their assigned expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level annexes.
Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Do not generate expressions with unresolved or invalid types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
Requirement Analyst Agent's role in traceability
